The terrain is raw and elemental, shaped by peat bogs and granite hills, with little to interrupt the horizon except occasional lakes, stone walls, and the sweeping curves of the road itself. It’s a place where the natural character of Wicklow feels at its most untamed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Visiting is about the journey as much as the destination. The drive through Sally Gap is an experience in its own right, drawing in photographers, cyclists, and film fans alike, with scenes from productions such as "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Braveheart"},{"type":"text","text":" and "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"P.S. I Love You"},{"type":"text","text":" filmed nearby. Many visitors stop simply to take in the silence and the feeling of being far removed from the everyday world."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Timing plays a huge role in how the landscape feels. Clear days reveal distant mountain ranges and dramatic light across the bogs, while mist, rain, or low cloud can transform the area into something moody and atmospheric. Early mornings often bring the quietest moments, with fewer cars and a stronger sense of solitude."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: Sally Gap is accessed via narrow mountain roads that can be exposed to strong winds, fog, and sudden weather changes. Parking is informal along the roadside, so caution is advised, especially in poor conditions."}]}]}

title: Wicklow's Guinness Lake View
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"High above a dark, crescent-shaped lake in the Wicklow Mountains, the Lough Tay Viewpoint offers one of the most striking panoramas in Ireland. Often called Guinness Lake due to its inky waters and pale sand shoreline, the view looks down over a dramatic glacial valley framed by steep slopes and the rugged bulk of Djouce and Luggala, creating a scene that feels both wild and perfectly composed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Unlike many scenic lookouts, Lough Tay’s appeal lies in its contrast. The deep, peaty lake sits sharply against the bright quartz sand at its edge, while the surrounding mountains rise abruptly, enclosing the valley and giving the landscape an almost theatrical intensity. Long associated with the Guinness family and the nearby Luggala Estate, the area carries a sense of exclusivity and mystique that adds to its allure."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Visiting is very much about pausing rather than exploring. Most people linger at the roadside viewpoint, drawn to the sheer scale of the view and the way light and shadow constantly reshape the lake below. It’s a favourite stop for photographers and road-trippers, with the landscape speaking for itself without the need for signage or formal trails."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Timing can dramatically change the mood. Bright sunlight sharpens the colours and enhances the famous “Guinness” effect, while overcast skies deepen the lake’s darkness and heighten the drama. Early mornings and quieter times of day offer a more peaceful experience, free from traffic and crowds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Note: The main viewpoint is located along a narrow mountain road with limited parking, so care is needed when stopping. There is no direct access to the lake itself from this point, and weather conditions can change quickly at higher elevations."}]}]}

title: Secluded Copper Coast Gem
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on the stunning Copper Coast, Ballydwan is a beautifully sheltered cove that feels like a local secret. You'll navigate a narrow, winding road down to a small car park, and from there, the dramatic reddish-brown cliffs and striking sea stacks are immediately in view. It's a peaceful spot, perfect for exploring rock pools, kayaking in the calm waters, or simply enjoying the impressive geology that surrounds you. Just be mindful of the tides and the warnings for rip currents if you decide to take a dip."}]}]}

title: Cosy Cafe with Exceptional Coffee
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in Tramore, Sunset Orange is a local gem where you can find some of the best coffee around, it's consistently smooth, balanced, and never bitter. Settle into the cosy atmosphere and pair your drink with a delicious cauliflower focaccia sandwich or one of their lovely cakes. It's the perfect spot to relax and treat yourself."}]}]}

title: Gorey's Specialty Coffee Gem
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on Esmonde Street, you'll find what many locals consider the best coffee in Gorey. Haddon & Sly is a gem with a wonderfully calm and cozy atmosphere, making it the perfect spot to unwind. The staff are incredibly welcoming, and alongside their exceptional specialty coffee, they offer a delicious selection of pastries and açai bowls."}]}]}

title: Historic Cliffside Beacon
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perched on the dramatic cliffs overlooking Tramore Bay, the Metal Man is a sight to behold. This striking figure was erected as a maritime beacon after a terrible shipwreck in 1816, and his outstretched arm still warns ships away from the hazardous coastline. The walk to see him up close is an adventure in itself, starting from Newtown Cove car park and following a rugged, and at times treacherous, cliff path for about 15-20 minutes. Be aware that access is complicated as it's on private land and the path is not maintained, so tread carefully, especially as the cliffs are unprotected."}]}]}

title: Historic Harbour Castle
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step back in time at this beautifully restored Norman castle, right on the edge of Dungarvan's charming harbour. You can explore the rare polygonal keep and the old military barracks, which now houses a fascinating exhibition on the castle's long history. The guided tours are fantastic for bringing the stories of the place to life, and the views from the top are well worth the climb."}]}]}

title: Copper Coast's Hidden Cove
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away along our stunning Copper Coast, Trá na mBó is a real hidden gem that feels like a secret cove waiting just for you. To get there, you'll take a beautiful cliff-top walk from near Bunmahon village, with incredible views opening up as you go. The final path down to the beach is a bit steep and winding, but it's a short trek of about 10 minutes and well worth the effort for the sight of the dramatic sea stack and towering cliffs that greet you. It's the perfect spot to escape the crowds and soak in some of the most spectacular scenery Waterford has to offer."}]}]}

title: A Fairytale Folly
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hidden within woodland near Lismore in County Waterford, Ballysaggartmore Towers present one of Ireland’s most unusual architectural curiosities. These elaborate Gothic style gate towers stand deep in the forest, originally constructed in the mid nineteenth century as an extravagant entrance to a grand mansion that was never built. Their scale and detailing create a striking contrast with the quiet rural setting that surrounds them today."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The towers were commissioned by Arthur Kiely Ussher as part of an ambitious estate vision. Although the planned house was abandoned due to financial difficulties, the ornate gate structures and associated lodges were completed, leaving behind an impressive but isolated monument. The result is a landmark that feels both grand and slightly enigmatic."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Access is via a clearly marked woodland loop of approximately two kilometres. The trail takes most visitors between thirty and forty minutes to complete and follows a well maintained gravel path through mature forest. Along the way, walkers pass a stone bridge and former gate lodges before the twin towers gradually come into view among the trees."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The surrounding woodland adds greatly to the atmosphere. In summer, filtered light moves through the canopy, while autumn brings rich colour to the forest floor. The quiet setting enhances the sense of discovery as the towers emerge unexpectedly from the landscape."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Parking is available at the designated trailhead, with information signage outlining the route. The path is generally accessible, though sturdy footwear is recommended during wet conditions."}]}]}

title: Historic Viaduct on the Greenway
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Spanning a quiet valley near Durrow in County Waterford, the Durrow Viaduct is a striking example of nineteenth century railway engineering. Its series of graceful stone arches stretch across the landscape, rising above farmland and gentle slopes, creating an elegant rhythm that contrasts beautifully with the surrounding countryside. Though no longer in active use, the viaduct remains an impressive reminder of Ireland’s industrial heritage."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Constructed to carry a regional railway line, the viaduct combines practicality with architectural refinement. Each carefully cut stone arch contributes to a sense of proportion and scale, and the height and length of the structure make it particularly notable when viewed from nearby roads or footpaths."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The viaduct is best appreciated from the surrounding fields and minor roads, where visitors can enjoy uninterrupted views of the full series of arches. Photographers and history enthusiasts often linger to capture the symmetry and the way changing light highlights the texture of the stone."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Seasonal and weather conditions enhance the experience. Bright days bring out the warm tones of the masonry, while mist or low cloud lends a more dramatic atmosphere. The surrounding farmland changes with the seasons, offering a slightly different perspective each time."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Parking is available informally along minor roads nearby, and visitors should respect private property. Climbing or walking on the viaduct itself is not permitted for safety reasons."}]}]}

title: Artisan Bakery & Cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nestled in Ireland's highest village, this beautifully curated artisan grocery, bakery, and cafe is the perfect stop. You'll find a welcoming atmosphere with wood-burning stoves and a lovely courtyard, ideal for enjoying excellent coffee and wholesome food. It's a true community hub, popular with locals and hikers, offering delicious sourdough, fresh pastries, and shelves stocked with local gourmet foods and organic vegetables."}]}]}

title: Waterford Greenway
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Waterford Greenway is a spectacular 46km off-road cycling and walking trail along an old railway line between Waterford and Dungarvan. This family-friendly route offers a beautiful journey through lush green countryside, featuring historic landmarks like the Ballyvoyle Viaduct and Tunnel. It's a perfect way to experience the scenic landscapes of County Waterford at your own pace."}]}]}

title: Roadside Cascade in Wicklow
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Plunging down a steep mountainside in the heart of the Wicklow Mountains, Glenmacnass Waterfall is one of the most striking roadside waterfalls in Ireland. Located along the scenic Sally Gap drive, the cascade drops in a long white ribbon through open moorland before reaching the valley floor. Its exposed setting gives it a raw and natural character, shaped entirely by the surrounding upland landscape."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Fed by rainfall from the Wicklow Mountains, the waterfall is at its most impressive after wet weather, when water surges down the rock face in powerful streams. In drier periods, the flow softens but still maintains its distinctive vertical sweep. The lack of heavy development around the site allows the scenery to remain largely untouched."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The main viewpoint is easily accessed from a small roadside lay by, making it a convenient stop along the Sally Gap route. Visitors can admire the falls from across the valley, where the full height and scale are best appreciated. There is no formal pathway to the base, and the terrain can be uneven and boggy."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Light and weather greatly influence the atmosphere. Bright conditions highlight the contrast between dark rock and white water, while mist and low cloud add a more dramatic tone to the valley."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Parking is limited and care is required when pulling off the narrow road. Visitors should remain on stable ground and avoid attempting to climb the surrounding slopes, particularly in wet or slippery conditions."}]}]}

title: Artisan Seaside Bakery
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Follow the scent of freshly baked sourdough to this wonderful local bakery, a true gem in Tramore. You'll often find a queue out the door, a testament to their incredible pastries and breads. Grab a coffee and a delicious morning bun, then find a spot to enjoy the sea views, it's the perfect start to any day."}]}]}

title: Enchanting Forest Boardwalk
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into what feels like a real-life fairytale at Ballinastoe Woods. You'll find yourself wandering along a now-famous wooden boardwalk that snakes through a dense, magical-looking pine forest. The main trail is a manageable loop, but the true highlight for many is the boardwalk section, which is just a 10- to 20-minute walk from the upper car park. As you emerge from the trees, you're rewarded with absolutely stunning views over Lough Tay, making the whole experience feel a world away from everything."}]}]}

title: Boutique Charm in Dungarvan
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Star Rating:"},{"type":"text","text":" 4-star boutique"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"What Makes It Unique:"},{"type":"text","text":" A stylish, design-led boutique hotel set within a beautifully restored historic building, blending heritage character with contemporary comfort in the heart of the city."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Overview:"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" The Hat Maker Boutique Hotel offers an intimate and characterful stay in Derry/Londonderry. Housed in a sympathetically restored property that nods to the building’s historic past, the hotel combines exposed brick, rich textures, and modern finishes to create a warm and sophisticated atmosphere. Its central location makes it an ideal base for exploring the city’s vibrant culture and famous walls."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Accommodation:"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Rooms are individually styled, featuring plush bedding, sleek en-suite bathrooms, and thoughtful design details. Contemporary furnishings complement the building’s original features, offering a balance of charm and comfort. The overall feel is cosy yet refined, perfect for a relaxed city break."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Dining & Experience:"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Guests can enjoy nearby cafés, restaurants, and traditional pubs just steps from the hotel, immersing themselves in the lively local scene. The hotel’s boutique scale ensures a personalised and welcoming stay."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Ideal For:"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"bold"}]},{"type":"text","text":" Couples and travellers seeking a stylish, centrally located base for exploring Derry/Londonderry’s history, culture, and nightlife."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"hardBreak"},{"type":"hardBreak"}]}]}

title: Modern European & Tapas Dining
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a stylish, modern bistro where you'll find a buzzing atmosphere centered around an open kitchen. This spot is celebrated for its inventive tapas-style plates with a strong Spanish influence, perfect for sharing and sampling. Don't miss the incredible dishes cooked in their special charcoal oven, which adds a unique smoky flavour to meats and fish, making for a memorable meal in the heart of the city."}]}]}

title: Stylish Bistro with Local Charm
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in the Linen Quarter, just a stone's throw from City Hall, Coco is a real local gem with a stylish, quirky vibe. You'll find the atmosphere relaxed and welcoming, perfect for anything from a special night out to a fantastic pre-theatre meal. The menu is all about beautifully cooked, locally sourced ingredients, creating modern dishes that are both comforting and creative."}]}]}

title: Vibrant Fusion Dining
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on Stranmillis Road, this spot is a local favourite for its vibrant, upbeat atmosphere and creative menu. You'll find a fantastic fusion of flavours, from incredible birria beef tacos to classic Italian pasta dishes and gourmet burgers. It's the perfect place for a fun dinner before a show at the nearby Lyric Theatre or just a lively meal out with friends."}]}]}

title: Modern Dining, University Quarter
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nestled in the city's leafy University Quarter, you'll find this stylish bar and grill offering a sophisticated yet relaxed dining experience. The menu is a celebration of Northern Ireland's best, focusing on fresh, local ingredients prepared with a modern, continental flair. On a fine day, try to get a table on the terrace overlooking the grand architecture of Methodist College – it's one of the best spots in the area to unwind."}]}]}

title: Creative Asian Fusion
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on a side street near the city hall, you'll find Yügo, a gem that brings vibrant, creative Asian flavours to the heart of Belfast. The place has a cool, modern vibe and is perfect for sharing a variety of inventive small plates, from fresh sushi to delicious bao buns. It's a popular spot, so booking ahead is a good idea to snag a table and experience some of the most exciting cooking in the city."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Sky-High Cocktail Bar
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For the most breathtaking, panoramic views of Belfast, head straight to the top of the Grand Central Hotel. On the 23rd floor, you'll find The Observatory, a sophisticated cocktail lounge offering a stunning 360-degree perspective of the city skyline. It's the perfect spot to unwind with an expertly crafted cocktail or indulge in a luxurious afternoon tea while watching the city buzz below."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Premier Steakhouse
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For one of the best meals you'll have in Belfast, you have to visit JAMES ST. It's a contemporary, brick-walled spot that feels both stylish and welcoming, specializing in incredible chargrilled and aged steaks sourced from local producers. The atmosphere is always buzzing, making it perfect for a special occasion or a memorable dinner where you can savor perfectly cooked dishes and fantastic service."}]}]}

title: A Wine Lover's Haven
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on the bustling Lisburn Road, you'll find this fantastic wine bar and tasting room, a real gem for anyone looking to explore new wines. You can sample from a huge, rotating selection using their cool self-serve machines or grab a bottle from the impressive wine wall to enjoy on the heated terrace. It's the perfect spot to unwind with friends, sharing some delicious small plates and a great bottle without the usual restaurant markup."}]}]}

title: Historic Maritime Luxury
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Immerse yourself in history at the Titanic Hotel, set in the beautifully restored former headquarters of Harland & Wolff, where the legendary ship was designed. You're right in the heart of the Titanic Quarter here, with stunning views of the slipways and the iconic Titanic Belfast attraction just a short stroll away. The hotel itself is a masterpiece of Art Deco design and maritime heritage, offering a truly unique and luxurious stay that connects you to the city's rich shipbuilding past."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Journalism-Themed Gem
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a unique corner of Belfast at The Reporter, a cozy bar that pays homage to the city's rich newspaper heritage. You'll find the walls adorned with vintage typewriters and old press photos, creating an intimate and friendly atmosphere perfect for a quiet pint or a lively chat. It's a real local favorite, known for its excellent selection of Northern Irish craft beers and spirits, and its welcoming spirit makes everyone, including dogs, feel right at home."}]}]}

title: Authentic Mexican Street Food
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a true taste of Mexico right on Ormeau Road, you have to check out La Taqueria. It's a vibrant spot where they're serious about authentic street food, even shipping specialty ingredients directly from Mexico to complement the best local Irish produce. You'll find the atmosphere is lively and casual, perfect for digging into some of the best tacos and margaritas in town."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Historic Victorian Pub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a piece of Belfast history at The Garrick, one of the city's oldest pubs. You'll find a classic Victorian hostelry with beautiful wood panelling and a welcoming atmosphere loved by locals. Whether you're settling in for some hearty pub food in the front bar or catching the live music in the back, it’s a perfect spot to soak up the city's authentic charm."}]}]}

title: Modern Italian Eatery
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on a central Belfast street, you'll find this gem of a modern Italian restaurant. It's the perfect spot for a memorable meal, whether you're sharing a few of their delicious small plates or diving into a bowl of freshly made pasta. The atmosphere is always warm and welcoming, making it a local favourite for everything from a casual brunch to a special evening out."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Legendary Music Hall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a real taste of Belfast's buzzing live entertainment scene, you have to spend an evening at The Empire. It's a fantastic, old-school venue set in a converted church, giving it a brilliant atmosphere and amazing acoustics for everything from rock gigs to comedy nights. You'll find three floors of bars and a friendly crowd of locals and students, all there to enjoy the top-notch music and comedy acts that grace the stage."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Lasagne & Ale Hub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked inside one of the city's best-loved pubs, The John Hewitt, you'll find this fantastic little lasagne joint. It's the perfect spot in the Cathedral Quarter for some seriously good comfort food paired with an amazing selection of local craft beers. The atmosphere is always buzzing, thanks to the live music and friendly crowd, making it a top choice for a relaxed and hearty meal before heading out to explore the rest of the city."}]}]}

title: Parisian Elegance in Belfast
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a touch of Parisian elegance right in the heart of the city, you can't beat Parisien. It's a beautiful brasserie with an old-world charm, serving up classic French dishes. You'll find it has a lovely, relaxed atmosphere, making it perfect for anything from a special dinner to afternoon tea. If you can, try to get a table with a view of City Hall – it's a stunning backdrop, especially when the Christmas market is in full swing."}]}]}

title: Stylish Bar on Malone Road
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on Malone Road, Post House is a beautifully designed spot where you can settle in for a fantastic evening. The atmosphere is always buzzing, whether you're grabbing a creative cocktail on the stunning upstairs balcony or enjoying a meal. It's a perfect blend of stylish and welcoming, making it a local favourite for everything from a casual date night to a special celebration."}]}]}

title: Authentic Belfast Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into the Sunflower Public House and you're stepping into a proper Belfast experience. It’s a no-fuss spot with a fantastic, friendly atmosphere, known for its great selection of craft beers and live traditional music that often fills the front room. Out back, you'll find a brilliant beer garden where they serve up some of the best wood-fired pizzas in the city. Don't be surprised by the security cage on the front door; it's a preserved piece of the city's history and a real talking point."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Legendary Comedy Hub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a guaranteed night of laughter, you can't beat Lavery's, Belfast's oldest family-run bar and a legendary spot for comedy. The club has an intimate and electric atmosphere, making it the perfect place to catch both local talent and big-name acts. It's a real Belfast institution, so grab a pint and settle in for an evening of top-tier stand-up."}]}]}

title: Authentic Belfast Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into The John Hewitt for a proper Belfast pub experience in the heart of the Cathedral Quarter. This isn't your average spot; it's an old-world pub with a big heart, known for its fantastic selection of real ales and hearty gastropub food. You'll often find the place buzzing with the sound of live traditional Irish music, creating a truly welcoming and authentic atmosphere that keeps locals coming back."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Go-To Cafe & Bar
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Right on Botanic Avenue, you'll find Town Square, a hip and friendly spot that's perfect whether you're after a morning coffee, a relaxed brunch, or late-night cocktails. The vibe is effortlessly cool and casual, making it a go-to for just about any occasion. They serve up fantastic, flavour-packed comfort food using local ingredients, and the drinks menu is one of the best in the city."}]}]}

title: Authentic Belfast Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a proper Belfast welcome, you have to pop into Maddens. It's a wonderfully traditional, no-frills pub where the personality is as big as the welcome and the Guinness is famously one of the best pints in the city. Settle into one of the cozy nooks and you'll soon be chatting with friendly locals, especially when the live traditional music starts up and the whole place comes alive."}]}]}

title: Quirky Pub in a Grand Hotel
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away inside the elegant Merchant Hotel, The Cloth Ear is a gem where you can escape the city's bustle. It’s a wonderfully quirky take on a classic pub, filled with vintage curiosities and a genuinely warm, convivial atmosphere. You'll find it's the perfect spot to settle in for a proper conversation over an inventive cocktail or a plate of elevated pub fare that feels both comforting and creative."}]}]}

title: Laid-Back Lisburn Road Pizza
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on the bustling Lisburn Road, Greens is a local favourite for a relaxed pizza night. You'll find a welcoming, laid-back atmosphere perfect for families or a casual catch-up with friends. They're known for their stone-baked pizzas, offering everything from the classics to more inventive toppings, and they do great pasta dishes too."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Oldest Tavern
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a piece of living history at what is said to be Belfast's oldest tavern, established way back in 1630. Tucked away in the charming Winecellar Entry, you'll find a cozy atmosphere with crackling open fires and traditional decor. It's the perfect spot to enjoy a creamy pint of Guinness and a hearty bowl of authentic Irish stew after a day of exploring the city."}]}]}

title: Cozy Local Pub Vibe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Nestled on Stranmillis Road, The Jeggy Nettle is a wonderfully cozy and welcoming pub with a real local feel. You'll find intimate booths perfect for a quiet chat, a great selection of drinks, and a fantastic atmosphere, especially when the live music kicks in. It's a dog-friendly spot too, so you might see a few friendly pups relaxing by the fire."}]}]}

title: Authentic Italian Dining Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a true taste of Italy right here in Belfast, you have to visit La Bottega on Lisburn Road. It’s a wonderfully cozy and lively spot, celebrated for its authentic dishes made with the finest imported ingredients. The staff make you feel right at home, whether you're popping in for a classic pasta dish or settling in for a multi-course meal. Don't leave without trying the tiramisu; it's a local favorite for a reason!"}]}]}

title: Historic Irish Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a piece of living history at Kelly's Cellars, one of Belfast's oldest and most authentic traditional Irish pubs, pouring pints since 1720. You'll feel the centuries of stories within its whitewashed walls, where United Irishmen once plotted rebellion. Settle in by the roaring fire, enjoy the lively traditional music, and savour what many locals claim is the best pint of Guinness in the city."}]}]}

title: An Authentic Belfast Pub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Down a historic cobbled alley in the Cathedral Quarter, the Duke of York is a proper Belfast institution. You'll feel the history the moment you step inside, with walls covered in vintage mirrors and memorabilia from the city's past. It's the perfect spot to grab a perfectly poured pint of Guinness, listen to some live traditional music, and soak up the unbeatable atmosphere with friendly locals."}]}]}

title: Live Jazz & Cocktails
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step back in time to a 1930s New York-style jazz bar right in the heart of Belfast's Cathedral Quarter. You'll find yourself sinking into plush red velvet seats, surrounded by art deco glamour, with the smooth sounds of live jazz washing over you every night of the week. It's the perfect spot to enjoy fantastic French-bistro dishes and expertly mixed cocktails while soaking up the sophisticated, intimate atmosphere."}]}]}

title: Vibrant LGBTQ+ Bar & Drag Shows
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Right in the heart of Belfast's queer quarter, you'll find this vibrant and welcoming bar, a real cornerstone of the local LGBTQ+ scene. It's the kind of place where you can enjoy fantastic cocktails, catch an amazing drag show, and feel the energetic pulse of the city. The atmosphere is always buzzing and inclusive, making it a perfect spot for a lively night out any day of the week."}]}]}

title: Historic Pub in Belfast's Entries
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in the narrow, historic Joy's Entry, Henry's offers a genuine Belfast experience that feels worlds away from the bustling city center. You'll find a classic pub atmosphere inside, complete with polished, old-school furnishings, perfect for settling in with a pint and some proper pub grub. The place is steeped in local history, named after the United Irishman Henry Joy McCracken, and often features live music that adds to its lively character. Whether you're grabbing a hearty meal like the steak and Guinness pie or just enjoying the drinks on the terrace, it’s a welcoming spot with a story to tell."}]}]}

title: Stylish City Centre Cocktails
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away near City Hall, you'll find this stylish, Art Deco-inspired spot, perfect for kicking off a great night out. It’s known for its fantastic artisanal cocktails and a delicious menu of international small plates that are perfect for sharing. The atmosphere is always buzzing yet feels intimate and cosmopolitan, making it a great place to settle in for a couple of hours with good company."}]}]}

title: Historic Pub in a Former Jail
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in the historic Joy's Entry alley, you'll find this absolute gem of a pub. The Jailhouse is set in a building that was once an actual jail, and they've kept so much of the original character, from the brick walls to the inscribed beams. It has a fantastic, vibrant atmosphere, especially in the covered outdoor area, making it the perfect spot to grab a pint and some tasty pub grub after exploring the city centre."}]}]}

title: Authentic Irish Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a real taste of Belfast, you have to pop into Fibber Magee's. Tucked behind Robinson's, it's the genuine article—a proper old-school Irish pub with a crackling fire and fantastic live traditional music every single night. It's the kind of place packed with local history and character, perfect for grabbing a pint of stout and soaking up the lively atmosphere alongside locals and fellow travellers."}]}]}

title: Funky, Art-Filled Cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into Harlem Cafe, a wonderfully quirky spot right in the heart of Belfast, beloved for its funky, art-filled interior and warm, welcoming vibe. It's the kind of place you can settle into for a while, whether you're after a hearty Irish breakfast to start your day or a delicious lunch. The menu is an eclectic mix of comforting local classics, and they're particularly famous for their fantastic Ulster Fry."}]}]}

title: 2 Taps Wine Bar
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in Belfast's vibrant Cathedral Quarter, this lively wine bar offers a warm, buzzing atmosphere perfect for sharing stories and delicious tapas, with a menu that sings with Mediterranean flavours. It features a spacious heated terrace, a popular year-round spot for an evening of good wine and company."}]}]}

title: Lavery's
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Belfast's oldest family-run pub, Lavery's offers a diverse experience with multiple bars, a large pool hall, and a roof terrace. It's a classic pub with a modern edge, featuring live music, comedy, sports, and a menu of traditional Irish pub grub and contemporary dishes."}]}]}

title: Historic Local Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into a proper local's pub in the historic Sailortown neighborhood, a place brimming with character and a welcoming spirit. You'll find it's the perfect spot to relax with what many claim is the best pint of Guinness in Belfast, especially after a day of exploring. The atmosphere is cozy and authentic, often filled with the sound of live music, from blues to folk, making it a true hidden gem for a memorable night out."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Craft Beer Haven
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into Belfast's original rotating taproom, a warm and rustic spot where the craft beer selection is always fresh and exciting. You'll find a fantastic array of local and international brews pouring from 14 taps, perfectly paired with delicious wood-fired pizzas and hearty comfort food. It's the ideal place to gather with friends, challenge them to a game of shuffleboard, and soak up the lively, friendly atmosphere."}]}]}

title: Belfast's Hidden Speakeasy
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away in a historic entry, you'll need to buzz for access to this hidden gem, a speakeasy-style cocktail bar that feels like one of Belfast's best-kept secrets. Inside, the atmosphere is intimate and cosy, perfect for settling in by the fire or heading out to the heated rooftop terrace. The cocktail menu is cleverly split into two 'bibles'—one with timeless classics and another with inventive, experimental creations, ensuring there's always a new temptation to try."}]}]}

title: Cosmopolitan Wine and Tapas
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a taste of Belfast's cosmopolitan side, you'll want to book a table at Ora. It's a vibrant, buzzy spot on Great Victoria Street where the focus is on creative, tapas-style small plates inspired by the chef's travels around the world. The dishes are perfect for sharing, arriving at your table as they're ready, which creates a wonderfully relaxed and social atmosphere. It's an ideal choice for a fun dinner with friends or a flavourful start to a night out in the city."}]}]}

title: Artisanal Colombian Coffee Roasters
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a truly exceptional cup of coffee, you have to visit Fidela Coffee Roasters. It's a cozy spot where they roast their own beans, sourced directly from family farms in Colombia, and you can really taste the quality and care in every sip. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, making it the perfect place to relax with a delicious pastry and watch the world go by."}]}]}

title: Artisanal Seaside Bakeshop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Right on the Portstewart promenade, you'll find this charming little bakeshop, a real local gem beloved for its incredible artisanal pastries and friendly welcome. The aroma of freshly baked goods greets you at the door, and the display of cronuts, cookies, and Portuguese tarts is hard to resist. Grab a specialty coffee and a treat to go, then find a bench along the seafront to enjoy them with a spectacular view of the coast."}]}]}

title: Elegant French Bistro
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on the bustling Lisburn Road, you'll find Marcel, a charming spot that brings a touch of French elegance to Belfast. It’s the perfect place for a relaxed yet sophisticated meal, whether you're catching up with friends or enjoying a date night. The atmosphere is always warm and inviting, and you can't go wrong with their classic steak frites or the rich beef bourguignon."}]}]}

title: Coastal Waterfall Escape
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Just a short stroll from the car park, you'll discover the charming Dunseverick Falls, a real local secret where a small river dramatically cascades directly into the Atlantic Ocean. It's a wonderfully peaceful spot, perfect for a few photos or a quiet picnic while you soak in the rugged coastal scenery. The walk from the lay-by is only about 5 minutes, taking you over a stile and onto the main coastal path to reach this beautiful sight."}]}]}

title: Specialty Coffee & Cocktails
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Right in the heart of town, you'll find Mo Chlann, a gem that feels like it has always been here. The name means 'my family' in Irish, and that's exactly the vibe you get in this warm, welcoming spot. They take their coffee seriously, serving up specialty beans, and on weekends, the place transforms into a lively cocktail bar, making it perfect for any time of day."}]}]}

title: Your Dublin Gateway
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Welcome to Dublin Airport, your gateway to the Emerald Isle. As you arrive, you'll find it's a bustling but well-organized hub, making your journey into Ireland a smooth one. Both terminals are within easy walking distance of each other, and you'll find plenty of shops and eateries to keep you occupied. For a true taste of Ireland before you even leave the airport, grab a pint and a bite at one of the restaurants serving up local fare."}]}]}

title: Secret Coastal Waterfall
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away inside a coastal cave, this stunning waterfall is one of Donegal's most magical secrets, but you'll have to work for the view. To reach it, you must park at the Largy Viewpoint and walk down through a private field to the shore, a journey of about 25 minutes. From there, the real adventure begins with a scramble over slippery, rocky terrain. It is absolutely essential that you only attempt this at low tide, as the cave is completely inaccessible and dangerous otherwise. The challenging trek is well worth the reward of seeing the cascade plunging into the sea cave."}]}]}

title: Authentic Irish Music Pub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"For a truly authentic night of Irish traditional music, you can't do better than Matt Molloy's on Bridge Street. Owned by a famous flutist from The Chieftains, this pub is the real deal, with music sessions happening seven nights a week in a cozy, intimate setting. It gets wonderfully crowded with both locals and visitors, so grab a pint, find a nook, and settle in for an unforgettable evening of world-class tunes."}]}]}

title: Westport's Live Music Hub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're looking for the real heart of Westport's music scene, you'll find it at the Porterhouse. It's a cozy, award-winning spot with wooden floors and an open fire, buzzing with locals and visitors every night of the week. You can catch a great traditional Irish music session here, with musicians often welcoming others to join in, creating a truly authentic and lively atmosphere."}]}]}

title: Charming Quay-side Deli Retreat
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away at the charming Westport Quay, you'll find this lovely little deli, a perfect spot to refuel with wholesome, freshly made food. It's a favourite for those coming down from a rewarding climb of Croagh Patrick, offering delicious coffee, gourmet sandwiches, and hearty soups that hit the spot. Grab a seat and enjoy the relaxed atmosphere, or get your order to go and enjoy it by the beautiful shores of Clew Bay."}]}]}

title: Authentic Irish Music Pub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"If you're looking for the real heart of Galway, step into Tig Cóilí. This is where you'll find the soul of the city in the sound of a fiddle and the warmth of the welcome. It's a proper local's pub, famous for its live traditional Irish music sessions that happen twice a day, every day. Don't be surprised if you find yourself in the middle of a spontaneous sing-along; it's that kind of place—cozy, lively, and utterly authentic."}]}]}

title: Michelin Star Dining on the Orient Express
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step back in time for an unforgettable meal inside two original Orient Express carriages resting on the grounds of Glenlo Abbey. You'll dine in an impossibly elegant setting, with polished wood, brass fittings, and views across the estate to Lough Corrib. The menu is a journey through the best of Irish ingredients, crafted with Michelin-starred precision that makes for a truly memorable and transportive evening."}]}]}

title: Lunar Landscape Hike
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into another world at the Burren, where a vast, moon-like landscape of cracked limestone pavement stretches to the horizon. This place has a quiet, ancient feel you won't find anywhere else in Ireland. You can choose your own adventure with trails ranging from short, gentle loops to challenging all-day hikes across the rocky hills. As you explore, you'll be amazed to find delicate, rare wildflowers and even orchids pushing up through the stone, adding splashes of color to the grey rock. The main trails start right from the Gortlecka Crossroads entrance, though parking is limited, so hopping on the free shuttle from Corofin during the summer is a great local tip."}]}]}

title: Your Central Dingle Hub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Settle into your perfect home base right in the heart of Dingle town. From here, you're just a short stroll away from the bustling harbour and all the best pubs and shops the town has to offer. The rooms are modern and comfortable, giving you a welcoming space to recharge after a day of exploring the stunning peninsula."}]}]}

title: An Authentic Irish Pub Experience
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into one of Dingle's most beloved institutions, a place that feels like the heart of the town. You'll find a classic, cozy pub on one side and a traditional leather shop on the other, a nod to its unique history since 1899. Settle into a snug with a pint of their own brew or choose from an impressive wall of Irish whiskeys, and soak up the genuinely friendly atmosphere where locals and visitors mingle."}]}]}

title: Dramatic Coastal Cliff Views
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Get ready for some of the most breathtaking views in all of Ireland at the Kerry Cliffs. For a small entrance fee, you can walk a short, manageable path from the car park—about 10-15 minutes—to the stunning, 1,000-foot-high cliffs. Many of us locals reckon they're even more spectacular than the Cliffs of Moher, and you'll find it's a much more peaceful experience. From here, you'll have the best mainland view of the mystical Skellig Islands and Puffin Island, making it a must-see stop on the Skellig Ring."}]}]}

title: Your Gateway to Belfast
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"As the main gateway to Northern Ireland, Belfast International Airport offers a straightforward and manageable start to your adventure. This airport only really caters for direct routes to UK and Europe but will be great to access flights to London/ Amsterdam /Paris to your next flight connection . You'll find it's conveniently laid out, making your journey from check-in to the departure lounge a breeze. Grab a coffee or a bite to eat at one of the various spots before you fly; it's a relaxed setting to get you on your way. Causeway Lounge is the best lounge to use here."}]}]}

title: Your Irish Adventure Starts Here
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Picking up your car here is the first step of your Irish road trip adventure. The process is straightforward, getting you behind the wheel and on your way to explore the winding country roads and stunning coastlines. The shuttle from the terminal is quick and frequent, so you'll be starting your journey in no time."}]}]}

title: Your Northern Ireland Adventure Starts Here
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"One of the Main rental car providers at the airport. I have used the company service myself so can recommend it as the choice of car rental from this airport. "}]}]}
locationName: Enterprise Car Hire - Belfast International Airport

title: Authentic Irish Music Hub
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Step into The Cobblestone and you'll instantly feel the heart of Dublin's traditional music scene. This isn't a performance for tourists; it's a genuine gathering of musicians sharing their passion in a cozy, unpretentious setting. Grab a pint, find a corner, and let the lively jigs and reels wash over you for an authentic Irish pub experience you won't soon forget."}]}]}

title: Vibrant Plant-Based Asian Fusion
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on Fountain Street, you'll find a gem that's completely changed the game for plant-based dining in Belfast. Jumon serves up creative and vibrant Asian fusion dishes that are so full of flavour, you won't even notice there's no meat. The atmosphere is always buzzing, making it a fantastic spot for a lively dinner with friends, complete with inventive cocktails and even a DJ on some nights."}]}]}

title: A Charming Garden Oasis cafe
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You'll find that Langtry Lane is a refreshing change from your typical garden centre. It’s a place crafted with genuine passion, where you can wander through thoughtful displays of seasonal plants before settling into the rustic café for some delicious, freshly made treats. It’s a lovely spot to spend an afternoon, with a great gift shop to browse and even a play area to keep the little ones entertained."}]}]}

title: Enniskeen estate mountain cottage
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The Mountain Cottage at Enniskeen Estate is one of the most unique and sought-after stays in Northern Ireland, perched high in the Mourne Mountains overlooking the Tollymore Valley and offering total privacy and seclusion. This beautifully restored traditional thatched cottage blends rustic charm with understated luxury, featuring a cosy open fire, a roll-top copper bath in the bedroom, underfloor heating, and a fully equipped kitchen centred around a wood-fired range. Outside, the standout feature is a private wood-fired hot tub with panoramic mountain views, making it the perfect spot to unwind after a day exploring. Designed as a true switch-off escape (no TV, just nature), it’s ideal for couples looking for a romantic, immersive stay, with hiking routes like Doan Mountain right on your doorstep. Expect a premium experience with prices typically starting around £250 per night, and a minimum stay often required."}]}]}

title: Cosy Local Coffee Stop
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Tucked away on Main Street, you'll find a gem of a family-run coffee shop perfect for a relaxing break. The coffee is exceptional, and the homemade cakes and treats are simply delicious. It's a wonderfully cosy spot with a warm, welcoming atmosphere that makes you feel right at home the moment you step inside."}]}]}

title: Countryside Farm Shop & Tearoom
description: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Just a short drive from Lisburn, you'll find this delightful, family-run farm shop and tea room, a true local favourite. It's the perfect spot to enjoy a hearty, home-cooked meal made with the freshest local ingredients before browsing the shop for artisan produce and unique gifts. The atmosphere is always warm and welcoming, making it a lovely countryside escape for a relaxing afternoon."}]}]}
